# Lessons Learned — 2026-06-16

**Date:** 2026-06-16  
**Generated by:** Hermes  
**Status:** final  
**Source window:** last 24h + current ops docs

## Executive snapshot

Today clarified the boundary between memory, daily summaries, lessons-learned notes, and the action tracker. The durable lesson is simple: save stable facts proactively, but do not turn every session into memory clutter. Daily summaries stay as audit trails, lessons-learned notes are the compounding layer, and only concrete follow-ups belong in the tracker. The new lessons-learned loop is the right place to capture small reusable process fixes without bloating prompt context.

## What we learned

- Hermes does **not** need a separate “daily memory” tier to be useful; the existing curated memory + semantic memory + session search + docs/tracker stack is enough.
- Stable facts belong in memory; tactical or evolving details belong in markdown or the tracker.
- Lessons-learned notes should stay short and opinionated; if there is nothing durable to say, a no-change note is better than padding.
- Operational surfaces only become useful when they are visible and linked; the internal dashboard should point to the live route, not just exist as a document.
- The first useful output of the loop is not more narrative — it is a habit of separating durable lessons from ephemeral chat.

## What worked

- Grounding the note in the daily operational summary, recent sessions, and tracker state instead of relying on chat memory alone.
- Keeping the lessons-learned contract small and explicit.
- Turning a process question into a documented loop rather than a one-off answer.
- Adding a dashboard link in the site footer so the control-plane page is easier to find and use.

## What to change

- Keep “memory” reserved for stable, broadly reusable facts.
- Keep lessons-learned entries compact and selective, not transcript-like.
- Prefer a single visible follow-up in the tracker over multiple loosely related notes.
- When a dashboard or control-plane page is created, wire discoverability at the same time.
- If a day produces no durable observation, write a short no-change note instead of forcing a lesson.

## Follow-ups

- No new tracker item created today.
- Existing visible follow-up: `AI-DASH-006` — Create Daily Lessons Learned dashboard card.
- Keep the lessons-learned index populated so the dashboard card can show real entries instead of a placeholder.
